# terrible_community_docker_docker_config (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `name` (String) The name of the config.

### Optional

- `data` (String) The value of the config. Mutually exclusive with `data_src`. One of `data` and `data_src` is required if `state=present`.
- `data_is_b64` (Boolean) If set to `true`, the data is assumed to be Base64 encoded and will be decoded before being used. To use binary `data`, it is better to keep it Base64 encoded and let it be decoded by this option.
- `data_src` (String) The file on the target from which to read the config. Mutually exclusive with `data`. One of `data` and `data_src` is required if `state=present`.
- `force` (Boolean) Use with `state=present` to always remove and recreate an existing config. If `true`, an existing config will be replaced, even if it has not been changed.
- `labels` (String) A map of key:value meta data, where both the `key` and `value` are expected to be a string. If new meta data is provided, or existing meta data is modified, the config will be updated by removing it and creating it again.
- `rolling_versions` (Boolean) If set to `true`, configs are created with an increasing version number appended to their name. Adds a label containing the version number to the managed configs with the name `ansible_version`.
- `state` (String) Set to `present`, if the config should exist, and `absent`, if it should not.
- `template_driver` (String) Set to `golang` to use a Go template in `data` or a Go template file in `data_src`.
- `versions_to_keep` (Number) When using `rolling_versions`, the number of old versions of the config to keep. Extraneous old configs are deleted after the new one is created. Set to `-1` to keep everything or `0` or `1` to keep only the current one.

### Read-Only

- `config_id` (String) The ID assigned by Docker to the config object.
- `config_name` (String) The name of the created config object.
